what_it_does: Declares the 'favour' interaction mark-type as a module-level MARK_TYPE dictionary, specifying its id, value shape, direction, and description for operator favour scores on gallery elements.
observations: The file contains no executable logic; it is a pure data/schema declaration.
The only exported object is the MARK_TYPE dictionary.
The mark id 'favour' is required to match the file stem.
value_shape is 'score' and direction is 'surface' (operator input).
The docstring frames this mark as the route-back of gallery:direction → territory_write → suite.mark.
It is described as additive and registry-is-truth.
standouts: A self-describing registry fragment for a single interaction mark type.
It explicitly ties the mark id to the file stem.
It documents the data-flow origin from gallery direction to suite.mark.
conventions: Uses a module-level MARK_TYPE constant as the registry entry.
Docstring explains domain semantics before the data structure.
Follows the convention that mark type id equals the file stem.
concerns: The requirement that id must equal the file stem is stated but not enforced in code.
notes: A small, declarative registry entry that defines how operator favour scores are represented as an interaction mark.
questions: What numeric range or scale is valid for the 'score' value?
How is this mark type registered and consumed by the suite/gallery system?
Are there constraints on when or where a favour mark may be written?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it defines the favour mark-type metadata.
intention_signals: To provide a canonical, human-readable metadata record for the 'favour' interaction mark so gallery and suite components can interpret operator favour scores consistently.
novelty: It encodes a bidirectional data-flow concept (gallery:direction route-back) in a single declarative mark-type record.
contribution: A declarative definition of the 'favour' interaction mark type, including its identifier, value shape, direction, and semantic description, which a fused system can keep as a registry entry for interpreting operator favour scores.
summary_for_embedding: mark_types/favour.py declares the 'favour' interaction mark type as a MARK_TYPE dictionary with id 'favour', value shape 'score', direction 'surface', and a description linking it to operator favour scores routed back from gallery direction.
intention_for_embedding: To register a canonical, self-describing metadata record for operator favour-score marks so gallery and suite components can interpret and write them consistently.
